description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: Symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped. Scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet. Workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.
symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.
scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet.
workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.

description: Symptom: Notification clients stop receiving updates, sessions drop, a core dump is recorded, and the REST service restarts. Scenario: This issue may occur when a subscription is added or removed at the same exact time notifications are being delivered. Workaround: Wait for the REST service to complete its automatic restart, then reconnect the notification clients and reapply the desired subscriptions.

description: Symptom: IP packets containing options headers bypass routed-in security policies configured with drop actions, resulting in policy enforcement failure. Scenario: When security policies are applied in the routed-in direction with drop actions configured, IP packets containing options headers are being processed by the policy engine but are circumventing the intended policy enforcement, causing these packets to be incorrectly forwarded through the system instead of being dropped as intended.
symptom: IP packets containing options headers bypass routed-in security policies configured with drop actions, resulting in policy enforcement failure.

description: Symptom: The CLI does not display Persona Access for interfaces such as 1/1/1 and similar ports, making it difficult to identify 10G/25G ports. Scenario: On the AOS-CX 10040 platform, which supports multiple speeds and split-port configurations, the CLI only shows Persona Uplink for 400G interfaces by default. There is no indication of Persona Access for other interfaces.
symptom: The CLI does not display Persona Access for interfaces such as 1/1/1 and similar ports, making it difficult to identify 10G/25G ports.

description: Symptom: Attempting to configure a class with a long name can result in errors such as "% Command failed," despite the same name working on a previous version. Scenario: This issue occurs when attempting to create a class with a name longer than 64 characters or have a class and DUR name with a combined length greater than 64 characters. Workaround: Use shorter class and/or DUR names.
symptom: Attempting to configure a class with a long name can result in errors such as "% Command failed," despite the same name working on a previous version.

Workaround: Use shorter class and/or DUR names.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what is the workaround for Classifier Bug 400270?
Classifier (Bug ID 400270): The documented workaround is: Use shorter class and/or DUR names.

description: Symptom: Devices with 80k static neighbor entries fail to boot after 30 minutes. Scenario: This issue occurs when attempting to boot a device with 80k static neighbors or attempting to copy a checkpoint with those characteristics to running configuration. Workaround: Reduce the scale or use the fixed firmware version.

description: Symptom: SNMP OID sysUpTimeInstance (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0) may return an incorrect value when polled, leading to inaccurate uptime reporting in monitoring systems. Scenario: This issue occurs on switches running AOS-CX 10.16 when using SNMP to query sysUpTimeInstance. The reported value does not match the actual system uptime, causing discrepancies in monitoring tools. Workaround: Use the OID hrSystemUptime (1.3.6.1.2.1.25.1.1.0) instead of sysUpTimeInstance for accurate system uptime reporting

description: Symptom: In a VXLAN environment, AOS-CXswitches may fails to install routes in the routing table, causing traffic loss for certain destinations. Scenario: This issue may occur during migration or configuration changes involving SVI and Routing enablement on VSX pairs. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing process using the following: Copy switch# start-shell
